title: Add structured output schema validation for agent responses
source: OWASP LLM09: Overreliance + LLM agent output validation best practices 2025
description: Agent output parsing in delegate.py and meeseeks.py uses regex fallbacks and loose JSON parsing without schema validation. A compromised or confused agent could return malformed output that bypasses sanitize_agent_output() checks. OWASP LLM09 recommends strict schema validation on all LLM outputs before acting on them.
proposed_changes: 1. In schemas.py, define JSON Schema definitions for expected agent output structures (e.g., DelegateOutputSchema with required fields: status, summary, files_changed). 2. Add a validate_agent_output_schema(output: dict, schema: str) function in security_utils.py that validates parsed JSON against the schema using jsonschema or a lightweight validator. Reject outputs that don't conform. 3. In delegate.py and meeseeks.py, after JSON parsing and before acting on results, call validate_agent_output_schema(). If validation fails, log the event and return a structured error rather than falling back to regex parsing of untrusted output. 4. Remove regex fallback parsing paths that could accept arbitrary unstructured content.
